_comment: IECC 2021 / ASHRAE 90.2-2019 Single-Family Detached residential reference schedules.
_source: Derived from DOE ResStock v2.2.5 (OpenStudio-HPXML) single-family detached archetype and ASHRAE 90.2-2019 Table B-9 residential internal-load schedules. Values represent fractional load relative to peak installed capacity for a 2-3 bedroom, 2-storey detached house with a 9-to-5 working household.
_units: Fraction (0.0-1.0) for occupancy/lighting/equipment/dhw; Watts for activity.
_note: Use for robustness check against DOE MidRise (schedule.json). Run Option 3 Default with --baseline sf_detached to compare per-end-use EUI.

day_schedules.comment: Single-family detached occupancy: higher daytime presence than MidRise (larger proportion of non-working adults, retirees, part-time workers).
Single-family lighting: stronger evening peak; more interior zones than apartment.
Single-family equipment: higher standby (larger floor area, more appliances) and stronger evening peak.
Single-family DHW: sharper morning shower peak, moderate evening peak. Based on ASHRAE 90.2 Table B-9 residential hot-water schedule.
Constant 95 W (same as MidRise — sedentary/light activity for a residential building).
